prompt: Our ticket-list projection ran for months against staging webhooks without a hiccup, then the first night on production traffic the batch summary job died: `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'email')` out of buildTicketRow, and the whole batch was lost — four thousand tickets, zero rows rendered. Production payloads are sparser than the webhook guide admits: tickets arrive with no requester block at all, contact can be literally null, org can be null OR missing OR `{}` without a name, brand-new tickets have no thread or an empty messages list, and metrics sometimes comes through as `{}`. The dashboard team needs the good rows plus a per-ticket problem entry naming the exact path that was missing (like `requester.contact`), not a dead batch. Fix ticket_view.ts so it survives the real-world variants — and please don't just sprinkle `as any` or `!` to shut the compiler up; it compiles fine today and still crashes at 3am. Repro and contract: `node --test test_ticket_view.ts`. Tests are read-only.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of ticket_view.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 5 pass / 10 fail: sparse payload tests throw TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'contact'), of null (reading 'email'), reading 'messages'/'body'/'sla', and the batch test dies wholesale instead of reporting per-ticket paths.
